title: CapabilityDelta
description: A neutral, target-agnostic capability delta in the invariant-kernel IR vocabulary. Every material spec for a change declares one; the analyzer composes it with the shipped baseline and every in-flight delta (the composition ledger) and checks the union against the signed invariant kernel. This schema names no target: nodes carry abstract roles, flows carry an abstract data-class label, and invariants are referenced by opaque id. The specific role names, data classes, and invariant ids are per-target DATA supplied through the kernel, never fixed in the core.

id.description: Stable id of the feature/spec this delta belongs to.

summary.description: Plain-language statement of the capability the change adds or removes.

stage.description: Where this delta sits in the composition ledger. 'cancelled' leaves a tombstone but contributes no active capability; 'runtime' is an observed-state delta for a conformance sweep.

nodes.description: Nodes introduced (or re-declared) in the capability graph: actors, stores, channels, egress targets, persistence locations, etc. — each identified only by an opaque id plus abstract roles.

id.description: Opaque node id, unique within the composed model.

roles.description: Abstract role labels this node bears (e.g. a sensitive-store role, an uncontrolled-egress role). Role vocabulary is per-target data defined by the kernel, not fixed here.

flows.description: Directed capability transfers introduced by the change. Each flow is a declared edge from a source node to a target node.

relation.description: Abstract relation label for the edge (e.g. read, write, export, link). Relation vocabulary is per-target data.

data_class.description: Abstract sensitivity class of the data on this edge (e.g. sensitive, restricted, metadata, non_sensitive). The concrete class set is per-target data, not fixed in the core.

rationale.description: Why this flow exists — the purpose that justifies the edge.

invariants_touched.description: The kernel invariants this delta interacts with, referenced by opaque id and degree. The invariant catalog is per-target data owned by the signed kernel.

id.description: Opaque invariant id as defined in the signed kernel.

degree.description: Exception-tolerance degree. D0 = absolute law (no exceptions); D1 = emergency exception with human approval, expiry, audit; D2 = signed config-as-data variant; D3 = evidence-required directive; D4 = advisory/interpretive.

implementation_surfaces.description: The concrete code/component surfaces the delta expects to touch, each with the relation the implementation is expected to exhibit — the target of the source-derived delta-fidelity check.

signature.description: Optional integrity metadata for the signed delta.
